title: Team-name matching does not normalize accents
evidence: normalizer.go:20 NormalizeTeam strips only '-XX' state suffix; normalizer_test.go:50 TestTeamMatches_NoFalsePositive asserts 'São Paulo' does NOT match 'Sao Paulo'. TASK.md:317-322 Data Quality Notes require handling accented names (São Paulo, Grêmio).
suggestion: Fold diacritics (e.g. golang.org/x/text/unicode/norm + runes.Remove) so accent-insensitive queries match.

title: ParseDate returns nil error on unparseable input
evidence: loader.go:14-28 ParseDate returns (time.Time{}, nil) when no format matches; callers (loader.go:98, tools.go:36) discard the error, so bad dates become zero-time silently.
suggestion: Return a real error on failure so date-range filtering and load diagnostics can distinguish missing from malformed dates.

title: Standings with empty competition pools all 5 datasets together
evidence: queries.go:173 GetStandings -> matchesForCompetition(db,"") returns allMatches (queries.go:33-34), so a season's table mixes Brasileirao/Copa/Libertadores/BR-Football/Historico, double-counting overlapping seasons.
suggestion: Require or default a competition for standings, or document that empty means combined.

title: Low token efficiency relative to output
evidence: scores.json token_efficiency=0.0751; ~1166 source LOC + 449 test LOC across 13 files.
suggestion: Informational — surfaced for cross-run comparison; no code change required.
